findings.title: CP-DDMM-004 and CP-DDMM-005 have no step field — controls unanchored
findings.detail: Neither CP-DDMM-004 (Daily MMS vs Payment Hub Reconciliation) nor CP-DDMM-005 (Periodic Review of Dormant Mandates) includes a `step` field linking them to the process steps they operate at. Three other controls (CP-DDMM-001 through 003) all carry step links. Unlinked controls cannot appear in the step-level control view and cannot be traced from the process flow. CP-DDMM-004 should link to PS-DDMM-005 (Register Mandate, where the MMS sync occurs); CP-DDMM-005 should link to the step where dormancy is reviewed.

findings.title: PS-DDMM-004 systems field is empty; SYS-DDMM-002 lists it — one-sided link
findings.detail: PS-DDMM-004 (Dual-Control Review) has `systems: []`. SYS-DDMM-002 (MMS) lists PS-DDMM-004 in its steps relation — the SME confirmed the Mandate Checker works within MMS. The link exists in one direction only; PS-DDMM-004 should reference SYS-DDMM-002 to complete the bidirectional trace.

findings.title: PS-DDMM-007 has no control linked despite regulatory-adjacent operations
findings.detail: PS-DDMM-007 (Handle R-Transaction) has no control element referencing it in the `step` field. R-transaction handling includes SL01 restrictions and mandate amendments, which are the subject of compliance gaps CG-DDMM-004 and PG-DDMM-006 (screening of amendments not assessed). Every other substantive processing step (PS-DDMM-002, 003, 004) has at least one control. The absence at PS-DDMM-007 is inconsistent and may reflect a documentation gap rather than a deliberate design.

findings.title: CH-DDMM-003 has no touchpoint elements linked
findings.detail: CH-DDMM-003 (Relationship Manager / Payments Service Desk) is documented as a channel but carries no `touchpoints` relation and no JT element has `channel: CH-DDMM-003`. The channel is described as an unstructured out-of-band support route, but without any touchpoint the client experience via this channel is invisible to the CX perspective and cannot be evaluated for friction or improvement.

findings.title: PS-DDMM-001 has no internal-role RACI entry — is intake fully automated?
findings.detail: PS-DDMM-001 (Receive Request) is assigned only to ROLE-DDMM-001 (Creditor Client) as R and A. No internal role — Mandate Clerk, Mandate Checker, Compliance, or Payments Ops Lead — is assigned even as Informed. If intake is fully portal-automated with no internal actor notified, this should be confirmed and the RACI documented as intentionally internal-actor-free. If any internal party is notified on receipt, the RACI needs updating.

findings.title: PS-DDMM-007 has no outbound transitions — is R-transaction resolution a dead end?
findings.detail: PS-DDMM-007 (Handle R-Transaction) has `transitions: []`. R-transaction resolution can result in mandate amendment (MD02 correction), mandate cancellation (AC04), or restriction application (SL01) — outcomes that may warrant a subsequent process step or loopback. If R-transaction handling truly terminates with no onward transition (e.g. no loopback to PS-DDMM-003 for amendment-triggered re-processing), that should be confirmed. If there is a missing transition, it should be added.
